detaileddescription.para: Reading within normal clinical range.
Reading outside normal range; clinician review recommended.
Reading at potentially life-threatening level; immediate intervention required.

briefdescription.para: Classification of a vital sign reading relative to clinical norms.

detaileddescription.para: The numeric ordering is intentional: ALERT_NORMAL < ALERT_WARNING < ALERT_CRITICAL, allowing comparisons such as if (level > ALERT_NORMAL). This ordering is relied upon by overall_alert_level().

briefdescription.para: Convert an AlertLevel value to a human-readable string.

detaileddescription.para:
level
The AlertLevel to convert.
One of: "NORMAL", "WARNING", "CRITICAL", or "UNKNOWN". Returned pointer is a string literal — caller must not free.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-007
Convert an AlertLevel value to a human-readable string.
Returns "UNKNOWN" for any value not in the enum to defend against undefined enum casts — important for IEC 62304 defensive coding.

briefdescription.para: Return the WHO BMI category string for a given BMI value.

detaileddescription.para:
bmi
BMI value as returned by calculate_bmi().
Null-terminated string literal (never NULL). Caller must not free.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-006
Return the WHO BMI category string for a given BMI value.
Uses half-open intervals consistent with WHO classification 2004.

briefdescription.para: Calculate Body Mass Index (BMI) from weight and height.

detaileddescription.para: Uses the standard formula: BMI = weight_kg / (height_m * height_m).

weight_kg
Body weight in kilograms.
height_m
Height in metres. Must be > 0.
BMI value, or -1.0 if height_m is zero or negative.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-006
Calculate Body Mass Index (BMI) from weight and height.
BMI = weight_kg / (height_m^2). Returns -1.0 for invalid (zero or negative) height to allow callers to distinguish an error from a legitimate very-low BMI.

briefdescription.para: Classify a blood pressure reading against clinical thresholds.

detaileddescription.para: Both systolic and diastolic values are evaluated independently. The more severe of the two classifications is returned.

listitem.para: NORMAL: Systolic 90–140 mmHg AND diastolic 60–90 mmHg
WARNING: Systolic 71–89 / 141–180 mmHg OR diastolic 41–59 / 91–120 mmHg
CRITICAL: Systolic ≤70 / ≥181 mmHg OR diastolic ≤40 / ≥121 mmHg

detaileddescription.para:
systolic
Systolic pressure in mmHg.
diastolic
Diastolic pressure in mmHg.
AlertLevel classification for the combined blood pressure reading.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-002
Classify a blood pressure reading against clinical thresholds.

Implements SWR-VIT-002 threshold logic. Critical thresholds are evaluated first (fail-safe ordering). If either systolic or diastolic is critical, CRITICAL is returned. If either is outside the normal range, WARNING is returned.

briefdescription.para: Classify a heart rate reading against clinical thresholds.

detaileddescription.para:
bpm
Heart rate in beats per minute. Negative values are treated as CRITICAL.
AlertLevel classification for the given heart rate.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-001
Classify a heart rate reading against clinical thresholds.
Implements SWR-VIT-001 threshold logic. Critical: bpm < 40 (severe bradycardia) or bpm > 150 (severe tachycardia). Warning: bpm < 60 or bpm > 100. Normal: 60 <= bpm <= 100.

briefdescription.para: Classify a peripheral oxygen saturation reading.

detaileddescription.para:
spo2
SpO2 as a percentage (0–100). Values outside this range are treated as CRITICAL.
AlertLevel classification for the given SpO2 reading.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-004
Classify a peripheral oxygen saturation reading.
Implements SWR-VIT-004 threshold logic. <90% = hypoxaemia (CRITICAL). 90–94% = mild hypoxaemia (WARNING). 95–100% = normal saturation.

briefdescription.para: Classify a body temperature reading against clinical thresholds.

detaileddescription.para:
temp_c
Body temperature in degrees Celsius.
AlertLevel classification for the given temperature.
Requirement
SWR-VIT-003
Classify a body temperature reading against clinical thresholds.
Implements SWR-VIT-003 threshold logic. <35.0 °C = hypothermia (CRITICAL). >39.5 °C = hyperpyrexia (CRITICAL). Values outside 36.1–37.2 but within critical bounds = WARNING.

briefdescription.para: Return the highest alert level across all vital sign parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Internally calls check_heart_rate(), check_blood_pressure(), check_temperature(), and check_spo2(), then returns the maximum value. This ensures that any single critical parameter elevates the overall patient status to CRITICAL regardless of other parameters.

vitals
Pointer to a valid VitalSigns structure. Must not be NULL.
The highest AlertLevel among all four parameters.
vitals != NULL
Requirement
SWR-VIT-005
Return the highest alert level across all vital sign parameters.
Implements SWR-VIT-005. Iterates all four checks and tracks the maximum. Uses a fixed-size array to avoid branch explosion and keep cyclomatic complexity low for IEC 62304 review.

briefdescription.para: Vital signs data types, thresholds, and validation functions.

detaileddescription.para: This module defines the core data structures and validation logic for patient vital signs. Each parameter is evaluated against clinically defined thresholds derived from AHA/ACC guidelines and classified into one of three alert levels: NORMAL, WARNING, or CRITICAL.

simplesect.para: Medical Device Software — IEC 62304 Class B compliant. All rights reserved.
